weather.description: Very hot — plan siestas, explore mornings & evenings

quickFacts: Temperatures can hit 40°C+ — carry water and seek shade midday
Tapas originated here — many bars still give a free tapa with every drink
The Alcázar is a Game of Thrones filming location (Dorne)
Don't eat dinner before 9pm — you'll be dining alone
foodScene: Free tapas with every drink at many bars — just keep ordering. Salmorejo (chilled tomato soup) beats gazpacho. Espinacas con garbanzos (spinach & chickpeas) is the local classic. Triana neighbourhood has the most authentic tapas bars. Fried fish at Bar Las Golondrinas.

places.name: Real Alcázar
places.caption: Moorish palace complex
places.description: Stunning Mudéjar architecture with tilework, gardens, and peacocks. You'll recognise it from Game of Thrones. Book online to skip the queue.

places.name: Seville Cathedral & Giralda
places.caption: World's largest Gothic cathedral
places.description: Christopher Columbus's tomb is here. Climb the Giralda tower's 35 ramps (no steps!) for panoramic views. The interior is staggering.

places.name: Plaza de España
places.caption: Semi-circular masterpiece
places.description: Built for the 1929 Expo, this tile-covered plaza is jaw-dropping. Rent a rowboat on the canal. Each alcove represents a Spanish province.

places.name: Triana
places.caption: Flamenco & ceramic quarter
places.description: Cross the bridge to the birthplace of flamenco. Ceramic workshops, tapas bars, and the Mercado de Triana food market. Best at sunset.

places.name: Metropol Parasol (Las Setas)
places.caption: Mushroom-shaped landmark
places.description: The world's largest wooden structure. Walk on top at sunset for 360° views with a drink included in the ticket. Roman ruins underneath.

places.name: Barrio Santa Cruz
places.caption: Former Jewish quarter
places.description: Labyrinthine white-washed alleys with orange trees, hidden plazas, and azulejo-covered walls. Get deliberately lost — it's tiny and charming.

places.name: Parque de María Luisa
places.caption: Shaded park oasis
places.description: Duck under the canopy of hundreds of trees during the midday heat. Beautiful tiled benches, duck ponds, and horse carriages.

places.name: Casa de Pilatos
places.caption: Hidden Renaissance palace
places.description: Mudéjar tilework rivalling the Alcázar but with a fraction of the crowds. The courtyard is one of the most beautiful in Seville.

places.name: Flamenco Show
places.caption: Raw art form
places.description: See authentic flamenco in a small tablao, not a tourist dinner show. Casa de la Memoria or La Casa del Flamenco for intimate performances.

places.name: Day Trip: Córdoba
places.caption: Mezquita mosque-cathedral
places.description: 45 minutes by high-speed train. The Mezquita's 856 striped arches are hypnotic. The Jewish quarter and flower-filled patios are unforgettable.

places.name: Triana District
places.caption: Ceramic quarter
places.description: Across the river — traditional ceramic workshops, Mercado de Triana food market, flamenco bars and calle Betis nightlife.

places.name: María Luisa Park
places.caption: City park
places.description: Former palace gardens with Plaza de América, doves, duck ponds and shady paths. Attached to Plaza de España.

places.name: Torre del Oro
places.caption: Golden Tower
places.description: 13th-century watchtower on the Guadalquivir. Small maritime museum inside. The golden tiles on top gleam in the sun.

places.name: Guadalquivir River Cruise
places.caption: River views
places.description: 1-hour boat cruise past Torre del Oro, Triana and the Expo 92 island. Evening cruises are atmospheric.
